quotaGuides.title: General Category MBBS Quota Guide
quotaGuides.summary: Overview of Open/General category pathways across AIQ, state counseling and institutional seats.
quotaGuides.overview: General category candidates usually compete in the widest pool, so strategy needs stronger rank-to-college mapping and backup planning.

sections.heading: Eligibility & Scope
sections.body: General category candidates can compete through AIQ and state quota channels based on domicile and counseling rules.

sections.heading: Application Strategy
points: Build tiered preference lists: dream, realistic, safe.
Use previous 5-year cutoffs to avoid over-concentration.
Track state-specific seat matrix updates round-wise.

sections.heading: Document Checklist
points: NEET scorecard and admit card
Class 10 and 12 mark sheets
Identity and domicile proofs as required

quotaGuides.title: OBC Category MBBS Quota Guide
quotaGuides.summary: Understand OBC reservation pathways, certificate requirements and counseling best practices.
quotaGuides.overview: OBC category candidates should validate certificate format and issuing authority early, as this affects seat entitlement during verification.

sections.heading: Eligibility & Reservation
sections.body: OBC reservation applies as per counseling authority norms. Central and state lists may differ, so verify list alignment before registration.

sections.heading: Critical Documents
points: OBC-NCL certificate in required format
Income and category support records if requested
Recent validity date per counseling authority

sections.heading: Counseling Strategy
points: Map OBC-specific cutoffs separately from UR cutoffs.
Keep fallback options in adjacent states if eligible.
Do not delay certificate corrections before round 1.

quotaGuides.title: SC/ST Category MBBS Quota Guide
quotaGuides.summary: Guidance on SC/ST reservation channels, verification readiness and list optimization.
quotaGuides.overview: SC/ST pathways can significantly improve seat access, but candidates should still maintain realistic round-wise preference planning.

sections.heading: Eligibility & Reservation Scope
sections.body: SC/ST reservation follows central/state counseling norms. The reservation share and applicable institutions vary by authority.

sections.heading: Preparation Checklist
points: Category certificate and supporting identity records
Domicile documents for state quota access
All originals and multiple self-attested copies

sections.heading: Seat Choice Strategy
points: Use SC/ST historical cutoffs to build realistic bands.
Prioritize clinical exposure and service obligations, not just brand.
Plan for mop-up rounds with updated vacancy tracking.

quotaGuides.title: EWS Category MBBS Quota Guide
quotaGuides.summary: End-to-end EWS counseling guide including certificate timing and eligibility guardrails.
quotaGuides.overview: EWS seat access depends heavily on valid, current documentation; expired or non-compliant certificates can block allotment.

sections.heading: EWS Eligibility
sections.body: Candidates must satisfy EWS criteria under applicable authority guidelines with valid certificate issuance window.

sections.heading: Must-Have Documents
points: EWS certificate in prescribed format
Income/asset declarations where requested
Verification-ready originals and backups

sections.heading: Practical Strategy
points: Validate certificate format before registration opens.
Track EWS cutoff drift in each counseling round.
Use balanced preference stacking across states and categories.

quotaGuides.title: NRI Quota MBBS Guide
quotaGuides.summary: How NRI quota seats work, eligibility interpretation and document preparation basics.
quotaGuides.overview: NRI quota counseling involves institution-specific rules and fees, so document clarity and financial planning are critical.

sections.heading: Eligibility Scope
sections.body: Eligibility may vary across institutions and states; review sponsor relationship and nationality requirements in detail.

sections.heading: Document Readiness
points: Passport, visa and sponsor documentation
Relationship affidavit/proof as required
Payment and fee commitment records

sections.heading: Selection Strategy
points: Compare total cost with infrastructure and clinical load.
Verify refund and withdrawal policies before locking seats.
Track institution-level notices daily during rounds.

quotaGuides.title: Management Quota MBBS Guide
quotaGuides.summary: Management quota process essentials including fee planning and college quality checks.
quotaGuides.overview: Management quota can widen options but requires disciplined due diligence on quality, obligations and affordability.

sections.heading: How Management Quota Works
sections.body: Management seats are generally offered under private/deemed institutions with separate fee structures and authority rules.

sections.heading: Evaluation Checklist
points: Total program cost and annual escalation
Hospital bed strength and patient inflow
Bond/service obligations and penalties

sections.heading: Decision Framework
points: Do not evaluate by seat availability alone.
Compare long-term ROI and postgraduate readiness.
Document every payment and communication.

quotaGuides.title: All India Quota (AIQ) Guide
quotaGuides.summary: AIQ process explained with rank strategy, state crossover opportunities and round planning.
quotaGuides.overview: AIQ allows interstate competition and can unlock high-value options beyond domicile states when preferences are structured correctly.

sections.heading: AIQ Eligibility
sections.body: Eligible candidates can participate in centralized AIQ counseling subject to governing authority guidelines.

sections.heading: Preference Planning
points: Construct rank-calibrated preference bands.
Include realistic alternates for each major city tier.
Use round-wise vacancy trends for updates.

sections.heading: Execution Tips
points: Finalize documentation before round 1 opens.
Track reporting timelines carefully after allotment.
Keep backup decisions ready for sliding/upgradation rounds.

blog.title: MedSeat Blog
blog.description: Practical MBBS admission guidance covering quotas, counseling strategy and college selection frameworks.

articles.title: How to Build a Practical MBBS College Shortlist
articles.excerpt: A structured framework to build dream, realistic and safe MBBS college lists using fees, cutoff history and seat trends.
articles.author: MedSeat Editorial

sections.heading: Start with Constraints First
paragraphs: Before comparing brand names, fix your non-negotiables: budget cap, preferred states and acceptable bond obligations.
This prevents overfocusing on unrealistic options and helps keep your counseling list executable.

sections.heading: Use 5-Year Cutoff Bands
paragraphs: Single-year cutoffs can be noisy. Build a range from multiple years and classify colleges into aggressive, target and safe.
Re-balance your list after each round based on vacancy movement.

sections.heading: Evaluate Decision Quality, Not Just Availability
paragraphs: A seat is only useful if training quality and long-term outcomes justify the commitment.
Review hospital load, infrastructure, bond penalties and total cost before final lock.

articles.title: State vs AIQ Counseling: How to Balance Both
articles.excerpt: Understand where AIQ helps, where state quota dominates, and how to avoid list duplication mistakes.
articles.author: Counseling Team

sections.heading: What AIQ Does Best
paragraphs: AIQ opens interstate choices and can improve option quality for strong ranks.
It is especially useful when your domicile state has intense local competition.

sections.heading: Where State Quota Still Wins
paragraphs: State quota often offers higher seat access for domicile candidates and better familiarity with local procedures.
State rules, category interpretation and seat matrix can materially change outcomes.

sections.heading: A Balanced Execution Model
paragraphs: Run parallel but distinct preference lists for AIQ and state to avoid accidental overlap and weak backups.
Treat each counseling channel as part of one integrated decision plan.

articles.title: Understanding MBBS Bond Rules Before You Lock a Seat
articles.excerpt: Bond years and penalties can alter real college value. Learn what to check before final choice.
articles.author: MedSeat Editorial

sections.heading: Bond Rules Impact ROI
paragraphs: A low-fee college with a high penalty may not always be the best financial choice.
Estimate total commitment including service years and potential penalty scenarios.

sections.heading: Verify Official Sources
paragraphs: Do not rely on forums alone for bond interpretation.
Always cross-check latest authority notifications and college prospectus updates.

infoPages.title: About MedSeat
infoPages.description: MedSeat helps MBBS aspirants make faster, better college decisions using structured data and practical guidance.

sections.heading: Our Mission
sections.body: We simplify high-stakes MBBS decisions by turning scattered admission data into a clear, comparable decision experience.

sections.heading: What We Focus On
points: Structured college details (fees, cutoffs, seats, bonds)
State/category/quota-aware discovery pathways
Decision support through comparison and fit-oriented insights

infoPages.title: Contact MedSeat
infoPages.description: Connect with us for support, corrections, partnership requests or product feedback.

sections.heading: Support
points: Email: support@medseat.example
Hours: Mon-Sat, 10:00 AM - 7:00 PM IST
Response SLA: within 24 business hours

sections.heading: Feedback & Data Corrections
sections.body: If you spot inaccuracies in college information, share official references and we will prioritize verification.

infoPages.title: How MedSeat Works
infoPages.description: From exploration to final choice, MedSeat guides you through each decision stage.

sections.heading: Step 1 — Discover
sections.body: Use state, category and budget filters to identify an initial pool of relevant MBBS colleges.

sections.heading: Step 2 — Compare
sections.body: Shortlist colleges and compare critical metrics like fees, cutoff trends, seats and bond obligations.

sections.heading: Step 3 — Decide
sections.body: Finalize rank-calibrated preference lists and align them with quota/counseling channels.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Answers to common MBBS counseling and MedSeat platform questions.

items.question: How often is college data updated?
items.answer: Data is periodically refreshed, and high-impact fields like cutoffs and fee structures are prioritized for update cycles.

items.question: Can I use MedSeat for both AIQ and state counseling?
items.answer: Yes. MedSeat supports planning across both channels and helps keep your choices organized by route and feasibility.

items.question: Are bond rules uniform across all colleges?
items.answer: No. Bond duration and penalties vary by state and institution, so always validate latest official notices.

items.question: How should I choose between two similar colleges?
items.answer: Use a multi-factor comparison: fee burden, cutoff stability, hospital load, bond obligations and long-term ROI.

items.question: Does MedSeat replace official counseling portals?
items.answer: No. MedSeat is a decision-support layer. Final registration, choice filling and seat acceptance happen on official counseling portals.

states.name: Maharashtra
states.intro: Maharashtra counseling is handled by the State CET Cell, with state quota seats across government and private colleges.

states.name: Karnataka
states.intro: Karnataka MBBS admissions are conducted by KEA across government, private and deemed colleges with multiple quotas.

states.name: Delhi
states.intro: Delhi has limited but highly competitive MBBS seats led by AIIMS Delhi, MAMC and other central institutions.

states.name: Tamil Nadu
states.intro: Tamil Nadu admits MBBS students primarily through state counseling with a strong network of government colleges.

states.name: Madhya Pradesh
states.intro: Madhya Pradesh MBBS admissions are conducted through state counseling with government, private and central institutes including AIIMS Bhopal.
